description_long: This object is a compact, cylindrical handheld flashlight, commonly used by law enforcement and security personnel. The body is textured for grip and features a prominent metallic button near the head, likely for power or mode selection. The head of the flashlight is slightly wider and has a crenellated bezel, which can serve both as a protective feature and for self-defense. The text 'POLICE 50000W' is visible on the body, indicating a high-powered model, possibly tactical in nature. The construction appears to be primarily metal, with some plastic or rubberized elements for durability and comfort. The overall design is robust, suitable for rugged urban environments and outdoor use.
description: A robust, black, cylindrical police flashlight with a textured grip, metallic button, and crenellated bezel, designed for tactical use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the flashlight's lens, crenellated bezel, and the 'POLICE 50000W' label on the textured grip.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the metallic button and the textured grip, with the head and tail cap visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the flat tail cap and the full length of the textured grip, with no visible controls.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the button and a small lanyard hole near the tail cap.
